title: TeC_BG_2D_Spin_up: v2.0.0
author: Lian, Taiqi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains spin-up procedures and both spatially distributed and plot-scale model configuration files for the manuscript: 
 Lian et al. (2025). A hybrid framework for the spin-up and initialization of distributed coupled ecohydrological-biogeochemical models. Submitted to Geoscientific Model Development. 
 The meteorological data is from the WSL website  www.wsl.ch/alptal  and from publicly available WSL data portal ( https://www.doi.org/10.16904/envidat.380 ). The data has been processed by the authors into the model input file ' Data_Erlenbach_run.mat ', which is archived at this repository. 
 Full workflow to reproduce the initialization and comparison procedure: 0. In the folder 'Spin_up_alp_track_flux', run the T&C-BG-2D model using 'Go_' functions, This generates simulation results with tracked lateral carbon and nutrient fluxes for all cells. Results for each year are saved in separate folders. 
 
 
 Run 'steady_compare_alp_steady_XXX.m' functions This produces 'Com_pools_alp_ice_allcell_XXX.mat', representing the steady state of soil biogeochemical pools, accounting for lateral carbon and nutrient transport across all cells. 
 
 
 Run 'RF_model_all_percentage.m' This generates 'models_alp_all_percentage.mat', which contains the Random Forest models trained with information from 10%, 20%, 40%, 60%, and 80% of the cells. 
 
 
 Run 'RF_model_all_percentage_predict.m', This produces RF_Ptm1_all_percentage.mat, containing steady-state estimates for all cells, either: directly from all cells, or predicted from Random Forest models trained with subsets of 10–80% of the cells. 
 
 
 In the folder 'Percentage_RF', run the T&C-BG-2D model again. This time, initialize the simulation with the soil biogeochemical steady states derived from the previous steps. Different initial conditions can be selected in 'ModelParams'. 
 
 
 In practice, the steps can be simplified as: 0. For the first T&C-BG-2D simulation (or other distributed ecohydrological model), it is not necessary to track lateral fluxes for all cells, a number of 10-40% of cells is recommended. 
 
 Obtain the steady state for the cells with tracked fluxes. 
 Train the Random Forest model using these cells. 
 Use the trained model to estimate the steady state for all cells. 
 Run the second T&C-BG-2D simulation, using the RF-derived steady states as initialization. 
 
   
 New in v2.0.0: 
 The current version of T&C-BG model is available at  https://doi.org/10.24433/CO.0905087.v4  and is updated regularly. The exact code and configuration files of plot scale simulation in the same manuscript is included in ' Plot_scale_simulation ' folder, one should follow the order of scripts: 
 
 Run 'Pre_spin.m' 
 Run 'Spin_up.m' 
 Run 'After_spin_up.m'

This repository contains spin-up procedures and both spatially distributed and plot-scale model configuration files for the manuscript: 
 Lian et al. (2025). A hybrid framework for the spin-up and initialization of distributed coupled ecohydrological-biogeochemical models. Submitted to Geoscientific Model Development.

In the folder 'Spin_up_alp_track_flux', run the T&C-BG-2D model using 'Go_' functions, This generates simulation results with tracked lateral carbon and nutrient fluxes for all cells. Results for each year are saved in separate folders.

 Run 'RF_model_all_percentage.m' This generates 'models_alp_all_percentage.mat', which contains the Random Forest models trained with information from 10%, 20%, 40%, 60%, and 80% of the cells.

 Obtain the steady state for the cells with tracked fluxes.

 Use the trained model to estimate the steady state for all cells. 
 Run the second T&C-BG-2D simulation, using the RF-derived steady states as initialization. 
 
   
 New in v2.0.0: 
 The current version of T&C-BG model is available at  https://doi.org/10.24433/CO.0905087.v4  and is updated regularly. The exact code and configuration files of plot scale simulation in the same manuscript is included in ' Plot_scale_simulation ' folder, one should follow the order of scripts: 
 
 Run 'Pre_spin.m'

 Run 'After_spin_up.m'
